Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. An Interesting Find: STM32 RDP1 Decryptor 33 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Show HN: Timber – Ollama for classical ML models, 336x faster than Python 164 upvotes, 29 comments
  3. An interactive intro to Elliptic Curve Cryptography 86 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Computer-generated dream world: Virtual reality for a 286 processor 129 upvotes, 23 comments
  5. Neocaml – Rubocop Creator's New OCaml Mode for Emacs 58 upvotes, 8 comments
  6. Right-sizes LLM models to your system's RAM, CPU, and GPU 216 upvotes, 49 comments
  7. AMD Am386 released March 2, 1991 38 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Show HN: Web Audio Studio – A Visual Debugger for Web Audio API Graphs 23 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. Plastic is made from milk and it vanishes in 13 weeks 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS Foundation 1241 upvotes, 423 comments
  11. Go-Native Durable Execution 35 upvotes, 6 comments
  12. Show HN: Omni – Open-source workplace search and chat, built on Postgres 100 upvotes, 26 comments
  13. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 63 upvotes, 16 comments
  14. Microsoft bans the word "Microslop" on its Discord, then locks the server 514 upvotes, 194 comments
  15. How to record and retrieve anything you've ever had to look up twice 116 upvotes, 39 comments
  16. Jolla phone – a full-stack European alternative 303 upvotes, 128 comments
  17. Mondrian Entered the Public Domain. The Estate Disagrees 108 upvotes, 40 comments
  18. Judge finalizes order for Greenpeace to pay $345M in ND oil pipeline case 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Making Video Games in 2025 (without an engine) 279 upvotes, 127 comments
  20. WebMCP is available for early preview 336 upvotes, 184 comments
  21. Libxml2 Enterprise Edition (AGPL, from the previous maintainer) 26 upvotes, 8 comments
  22. /e/OS is a complete "deGoogled", mobile ecosystem 409 upvotes, 240 comments
  23. Anthropic Cowork feature creates 10GB VM bundle on macOS without warning 65 upvotes, 29 comments
  24. First-ever in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. U.S. science agency moves to restrict foreign scientists from its labs 235 upvotes, 179 comments
  26. OpenClaw Surpasses React to Become the Most-Starred Software Project on GitHub 83 upvotes, 57 comments
  27. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 379 upvotes, 324 comments
  28. Apple introduces the new iPad Air, powered by M4 61 upvotes, 50 comments
  29. Use the Mikado Method to do safe changes in a complex codebase 13 upvotes, 8 comments
  30. How to talk to anyone and why you should 276 upvotes, 401 comments
